title: Inhomogeneous Coulomb Models Without Defects are Sick: Domain Wall Energies Scaling as Volume (not Boundary Area)
summary: This work demonstrates a fundamental pathology in Coulomb lattice models: domain walls cost energy proportional to system volume rather than just boundary area. The authors prove this using the six vertex model and show that even when macroscopic constraints are met, boundaries impose extensive free energy costs, highlighting the critical role of boundary conditions in these systems.
main_problem: The authors investigate pathological behavior in Coulomb models where domain wall energies scale extensively with system volume rather than merely with boundary area, even when macroscopic constraints are satisfied.
main_result:
Generic domain walls cost energy proportional to the system volume, and even boundaries satisfying $
abla \cdot B \cdot n = 0$ can impose an extensive free energy cost on the Helmholtz free energy.
method: The analysis uses theoretical techniques applied to exactly solvable lattice models, comparing partition functions under different boundary conditions (periodic vs. domain wall).
model_or_system:
The study applies to general lattice systems obeying a divergence-free constraint ($
abla \cdot \langle B angle = 0$), with specific examples including the square lattice six vertex model, quantum dimers, and classical spin ice.
key_observables: Domain wall energy scaling (Volume vs. Area), Helmholtz free energy cost, monopole density, and partition functions for different boundary conditions.
important_parameters:
The divergence-free constraint ($
abla \cdot \langle B angle = 0$), the ratio of ground state pseudo-magnetic fields across a domain wall ($\left\langle \mathbf{B}_{R}ightangle -\left\langle \mathbf{B}_{L}ightangle$), and temperature/coupling constants.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on coarse-graining variables $\langle B angle$ related to microscopic degrees of freedom, and the comparison between different boundary condition ensembles (periodic vs. domain wall).
figures_summary: Figures illustrate non-vanishing divergence at interfaces, six vertex configurations for the model, and comparisons of partition functions under various boundary conditions.
paper_structure: The paper establishes the general problem using Coulomb models, analyzes specific examples like the six vertex model to demonstrate volume scaling pathologies, and concludes by showing that even seemingly compatible boundaries have thermodynamic consequences.
why_interesting: While focused on condensed matter statistical mechanics, the concept of boundary-induced extensive energy costs is relevant for understanding finite-size effects in quantum simulations or open quantum systems where boundary conditions are crucial.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
